learning_outcomes:
Major Programme: Major Programme: The key expected learning outcomes (i.e.
knowledge, skills, and values/attitude, for graduates of the Bachelor of
Chinese Medicine programme) are as follows: Knowledge Outcomes Graduates will have possessed broad and
in-depth knowledge related to Chinese Medicine, including the following: (1) basic concepts and historical development of
Chinese medicine; (2) basic principles underpinning the use of
Chinese materia medica and herbal formulary; (3) basic principles underpinning the practice of
acupuncture and moxibustion; (4) etiology, pathomechanisms of common diseases
in clinical specialties of Chinese medicine; (5) Chinese medicine classics; (6) fundamental concepts in life sciences; (7) basic principles of diagnostics and
therapeutics in Western medicine; and (8) basic concepts of medical statistics. Skills Outcomes Generic competencies Graduates should acquire generic competences,
including the following: (1) communication and interpersonal skills; (2) presentation skills for succinctly and clearly
communicating medical and scientific findings to an audience of professionals
or lay persons; (3) competence in utilizing computer and the
internet to search for information; and (4) analytical skills and critical thinking for
problem solving. Professional skills Graduates should have full competence in
professional skills, including the following: (1) diagnostic methods and pattern differentiation
in Chinese medicine; (2) formulating treatment strategies based on
disease diagnosis and pattern differentiation; (3) applying common Chinese herbs and formulas for
treatment of diseases; (4) locating common acupuncture points and
practicing acupuncture and moxibustion techniques in clinical setting; (5) mastering tuina manual techniques and their
application in treatment; (6) ability to manage a Chinese medicine clinic as
a business; and (7) information management skills such as
inputting and retrieval of medical data. Attitude/Value outcomes Graduates should have a positive attitude
towards issues concerning Chinese Medicine practice and development,
including the following: (1) conforming to professional ethics and codes of
conduct; (2) an awareness of the latest developments and
advancements in Chinese medicine; (3) recognizing the strengths and limitations of
Chinese and Western medicine; (4) recognizing the need for further personal and
professional development; and (5) ability in adapting to the changing social and
health-care environment so as to stay competitive.
study_scheme:
Chinese Medicine Chinese Medicine Applicable to
students admitted in 2023-24
Major Programme Requirement The Programme consists of two parts: the Pre-clinical
Programme (4.5 years) and the Clinical Programme (1.5 years). Pre-clinical Programme In the Pre-clinical Programme (4.5 years), students have
to complete both required and elective courses including subjects in basic
medical and life sciences, Chinese medical concepts and principles of
Chinese medicine application, Chinese medicine classics and history,
specialties in Chinese medicine and clinical training. Students who take
“Clinical Training” courses (i.e. BCME 2401-2405) may need to undergo
training in summer vacation. Clinical Programme The Clinical Programme extends over 1.5 years. The first
year provides students with teaching in clinical settings of clinics and
wards in a Chinese medicine hospital in the Mainland. Clinical training
will cover various specialties in Chinese medicine including internal
medicine, acupuncture and bone setting. Students will rotate among
attachments to various clinical departments and write reports on case histories
of selected topics. During the last six months, students will receive advanced
clinical training in one or two selected specialties by attaching to a Chinese
medicine hospital in the Mainland and/ or a specialty clinic in Hong
Kong. They will also learn about the operation of Chinese medicine clinics
in Hong Kong and the diagnosis and treatment of common medical problems
seen in out-patient clinic settings. A professional examination
will be arranged for students at the end of each term to assess their
academic performance. Students may need to undergo training during summer and
winter vacations.
Students are required to
complete a minimum of 157 units of courses as follows:

Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  All BCME courses as well as those labeled as # will
be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Graduates of the Programme will be qualified to sit
for the Chinese Medicine Practitioners Licensing Examination conducted by
the Hong Kong Chinese Medicine Council. Those who pass the Examination are
eligible to become a registered Chinese Medicine Practitioner in Hong
Kong.
